page: Microsoft Azure FX24-12ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, featuring 6 physical cores and 12 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 504.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core. It operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. There are no bundled GPUs or local storage drives, though the instance is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core execution with a score of 2249.0 and text processing at 2988.0, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression at 34.73 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.22 GB/sec. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average range, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 13337.12 and a static web serving throughput of 22.97 GB/sec. However, the Passmark memory score is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25% at 1619.71.

Given the massive 504.0 GB memory allocation relative to its 12 vCPUs, this server is highly optimized for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. It is well-suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, which achieved 18.88 million SET operations per second in testing, and memory-heavy enterprise applications. The high-frequency 4.2 GHz CPU provides excellent single-threaded performance, making it cost-effective for workloads where licensing is core-bound but memory demands are exceptionally high.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX24-12ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server from the FXmsv2 family, featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 6 physical cores and 12 vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz. It is configured with 504.0 GB of RAM, providing a substantial 42.0 GB of memory per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU resources. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2249.0 and strong single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, its Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile makes the server highly cost-effective for memory-intensive, core-licensed applications, such as in-memory databases and Redis caching layers, where high memory capacity is required alongside strong single-threaded CPU performance.

tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual server delivering massive RAM capacity per core alongside strong single-threaded processing performance.

504.0 GB of RAM providing 42.0 GB of memory per core
